name: Amitabha Buddha
shortDescription: Amitabha Buddha is a celestial buddha in Mahayana Buddhism, revered for his infinite merit and compassion. He presides over the Pure Land (Sukhavati), a realm of bliss and enlightenment.
description: Amitabha Buddha, also known as Amida Buddha in Japanese and O-mi-to Fo in Chinese, is a central figure in Pure Land Buddhism, one of the most widely practiced branches of Mahayana Buddhism. His name, Amitabha, translates to "Infinite Light" and "Infinite Life," reflecting his boundless compassion and the eternal nature of his enlightened existence. According to the Larger Sutra of Immeasurable Life, Amitabha was once a king who renounced his throne and became a monk named Dharmakara. He made forty-eight vows to save all beings, promising to create a Pure Land where they could easily attain enlightenment. Through countless eons of virtuous practice, he fulfilled these vows and became Amitabha Buddha, the ruler of Sukhavati, the Western Paradise. Devotees believe that by sincerely reciting his name (Nianfo), they can be reborn in the Pure Land, free from suffering and close to enlightenment. Amitabha is often depicted in serene meditation, holding a lotus flower, symbolizing purity and spiritual awakening. His presence is a source of hope and refuge for those seeking liberation from the cycle of rebirth. The practice of reciting Amitabha's name is a simple yet profound path to salvation, emphasizing faith and devotion as means to achieve enlightenment. His influence extends throughout East Asia, where he is venerated as a compassionate savior who offers solace and guidance to all beings. The Pure Land teachings associated with Amitabha offer a path accessible to everyone, regardless of their social status or intellectual capacity.

sources.title: The Larger Sutra of Immeasurable Life
